name: One Seattle Plan: Centers & Corridors Rezones
summary: Zoning legislation transmitted to City Council in January 2026 to implement the One Seattle Plan growth strategy: rezones in 29 new Neighborhood Centers plus a 30th in South Park, five Urban Centers with expanded boundaries (Greenwood, Queen Anne, Graham, Admiral, and Morgan Junction), a new Pinehurst–Haller Lake Urban Center, and along frequent transit routes. Most rezones allow 5–6 story apartments and condos, with MHA affordability requirements applying to all rezoned properties; Lowrise and Midrise development standards are also updated, including a new 6-story MR zone. Rezones of Seattle's seven regional centers, such as Downtown and Capitol Hill, follow in a later phase.

name: Citywide ADU Reform
summary: Seattle allows two accessory dwelling units per residential lot with no owner-occupancy requirement, adopted in July 2019 as Ordinance 125854 (effective August 8, 2019). Ordinance 127211, signed May 2025, further eased ADU size and siting barriers to comply with Washington's statewide HB 1337 ADU law.

name: Seattle Comprehensive Plan 2044 / One Seattle Plan
summary: Seattle's Comprehensive Plan 2044, adopted December 2025, sets land use and growth policies through 2044. The first wave of implementing legislation — Ordinance 127376 — repealed and replaced SMC Chapter 23.44, collapsing NR1/NR2/NR3 into a unified Neighborhood Residential zone that allows houses, townhomes, and up to 6-unit multiplexes by right (9 with stacked-flat bonuses). Centers and Corridors follow-on legislation is rolling out through 2026. Required under Washington State's Growth Management Act and HB 1110 middle-housing law.

name: Ballard Link Extension Station Planning
summary: Early planning for zoning and land use around future Ballard Link Light Rail extension stations. Sound Transit's expansion will add new stations in Interbay, Smith Cove, and Ballard, requiring updates to local zoning to support transit-oriented development.

name: Industrial & Maritime Strategy Update
summary: First major overhaul of Seattle's industrial and maritime zoning in 35 years, passed July 2023 and effective October 23, 2023. Created three new zones: Maritime, Manufacturing & Logistics (MML) protecting core industrial lands; Industry & Innovation (II) allowing dense multi-story industrial-plus-office development near light rail; and Urban Industrial (UI) supporting small-scale industry with limited housing and retail at neighborhood edges.
